description: Hypothesis-level starter rules for anime livery cleanup research. These are not verified rules.

rules.claim: Eye regions should preserve crisp silhouette, eyelid boundaries, and readable eye direction instead of chasing soft pixel patches.
rules.strategy: Favor a small number of sharp, intentional shapes for eyelids and eye boundaries.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.

rules.claim: Iris and pupil regions should use clear nested structure rather than scattered small blobs.
rules.strategy: Use clean centered shapes for iris/pupil when roundness is intentional, with limited small detail shapes.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.

rules.claim: Eye highlights should remain simple, bright, and readable instead of becoming many tiny near-white fragments.
rules.strategy: Use one or two deliberate highlight shapes and avoid fragmented sparkle noise.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.

rules.claim: Eyebrow and upper eyelid regions should read as clean directional strokes.
rules.strategy: Use narrow directional shapes and avoid soft blob approximations.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.

rules.claim: Hair tip regions should avoid visible circle or ellipse blob clusters.
rules.strategy: Use angular base shapes and trimming/cutter shapes to preserve sharp tips.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.

rules.claim: Hair strand edges should avoid many tiny patches that make the silhouette look noisy.
rules.strategy: Prefer fewer directional shapes that describe the strand edge cleanly.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.

rules.claim: Face outlines should be smooth and intentional, not made of obvious soft blobs or jittery fragments.
rules.strategy: Use clean curve or edge shapes where verified; avoid noisy patch repair around the face silhouette.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.

rules.claim: Mouth regions should preserve expression readability rather than small pixel-level color fragments.
rules.strategy: Favor a simple readable mouth curve or line with minimal supporting shadow shapes.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.

rules.claim: Clothing shadow blocks should use larger clean regions instead of many near-identical micro layers.
rules.strategy: Merge visual intent into fewer hard-edge shadow blocks when the silhouette allows it.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.

rules.claim: Glow and disk primitives can reduce anime clarity in small character-detail regions.
rules.strategy: Use glow/disk only when softening is intentional; prefer hard-edge construction for eyes, hair tips, mouth, and face outlines.
rules.notes: Initial hypothesis. Needs real case validation.
